get.description:
Liveness check: "is the process alive and able to answer requests at
all". `config_loaded`/`cache_loaded` are cheap, synchronous checks that
Task 11's Tauri startup gate needs to know the backend can actually do
work (not just that the HTTP server is up) -- see the design note in
docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-20-desktop-frontend.md Task 1: this stays
liveness-flavored on purpose; deeper readiness checks (translator API key
validity, network reachability) belong in a future `/ready` endpoint, not
more fields bolted onto `/health`.

Both checks swallow their own exceptions -- a corrupt config.yaml or
cache.json must never turn a liveness probe into a 500.

Per-file metadata for one tracked repo's `.md` files: `status`
(`"synced"` if the cached blob_hash matches the current one, else
`"pending"`), `last_sync` (the cached `translated_at`, if any), and
`error` (the file's persisted `last_error.message`, if any).

Never returns `status: "running"` -- that's a frontend-only overlay
sourced from the `/logs` WebSocket's `file_start`/`file_translated`/
`file_failed` events, not from this read-only REST snapshot. A file can
be `"pending"` AND carry a non-null `error` simultaneously (failed last
attempt, still due for retry) -- that's correct, not a bug.

Read-only: resolves the repo's local checkout path the same way
`git_manager.clone_or_pull` does, but never clones/pulls. If the repo
has never been synced (no local checkout / `.git` dir yet), returns `[]`
rather than shelling out to `git` on a nonexistent path.

schema.title: Response List Repo Files Repos  Name  Files Get
Response Sync Repo Endpoint Repos  Name  Sync Post
Response Sync File Endpoint Repos  Name  Files  Path  Sync Post
Response Sync All Endpoint Repos Sync All Post
Response Cancel Sync All Repos Sync All Cancel Post

x-health-response-shape._comment: Hand-documented because /health (like /config, /repos, etc.) returns a plain dict, so FastAPI/OpenAPI only ever generates a generic additionalProperties:true object schema for it -- the actual field names below are NOT visible in the paths['/health'] entry above. This section exists because Task 1 of docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-20-desktop-frontend.md enriched /health beyond {"status": "ok"} and the field names matter for desktop/src/api.ts's typed health() return type. Read directly from repo_translator/api_server.py's health() function (captured 2026-06-20) and confirmed by live curl against a running instance, not guessed.

status.notes: Always "ok" -- this route only returns 200; there is no failure status value.

config_loaded.notes: True iff repo_translator.config.load_config() did not raise. Exceptions (e.g. a corrupt config.yaml) are caught internally and reported as false; they never bubble into a 500.

cache_loaded.notes: True iff repo_translator.cache_manager.load(cache_manager.DEFAULT_CACHE_PATH) did not raise. Same exception-swallowing behavior as config_loaded.

version.notes: importlib.metadata.version("repo-translator"), i.e. the installed package version from pyproject.toml's [project] version field (currently "0.1.0"). Falls back to the literal string "unknown" if package metadata cannot be found (e.g. running from an unusual environment where the package was never pip/uv-installed).

x-health-response-shape.source_reference: repo_translator/api_server.py, health() function (as of commit at time of this snapshot). Verify against current source if this file appears stale.

x-config-response-shape._comment: Hand-documented because /config (like /health, /repos, etc.) returns a plain dict, so FastAPI/OpenAPI only ever generates a generic additionalProperties:true object schema for it -- the field names below are NOT visible in paths['/config'] above. This section exists because Task 6 of docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-20-desktop-frontend.md added an optimistic-concurrency `revision` field to AppConfig, and Task 7 changed translator.api_key's exposure to a boolean api_key_set (see x-config-api-key-write-semantics below). Read directly from repo_translator/config.py's AppConfig model and repo_translator/api_server.py's get_config()/put_config()/_serialize_config() functions and confirmed by live curl against a scratch-REPO_TRANSLATOR_HOME instance, not guessed.

revision.notes: Optimistic-concurrency token. GET /config returns the server's current revision. PUT /config's request body must include the `revision` the client last read; save_config() increments it by 1 on every write (including the very first save of a fresh default config, so revision goes 0 -> 1 on that write). If the PUT payload's revision does not match the server's current revision at request time, the server returns 409 with body {"detail": "\u914d\u7f6e\u5df2\u66f4\u65b0\uff0c\u8bf7\u91cd\u65b0\u52a0\u8f7d\u540e\u91cd\u8bd5"} and does NOT write -- the client must re-GET and redo its edit rather than retry with the stale payload. Scope limit: this is a single-process load->compare->write check, not a cross-process compare-and-swap (see the docstring next to AppConfig.revision in config.py); it protects two screens racing within one running desktop app, not the CLI watch daemon and the desktop sidecar writing the same config.yaml concurrently.

x-config-response-shape.source_reference: repo_translator/config.py (AppConfig.revision, save_config()) and repo_translator/api_server.py (get_config(), put_config()), as of Task 6 of docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-20-desktop-frontend.md. Verify against current source if this file appears stale.

x-config-api-key-write-semantics._comment: Hand-documented for the same reason as x-config-response-shape (plain-dict route, OpenAPI gives no field-level schema). Task 7 of docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-20-desktop-frontend.md changed translator.api_key's exposure: the GET /config response (and PUT's response, since put_config() returns the same _serialize_config() shape) NEVER includes the literal api_key value -- it is replaced by a boolean api_key_set. This is a breaking response-shape change from the prior snapshot (which showed translator.api_key directly, e.g. omitted via exclude_none when null). Read directly from repo_translator/api_server.py's _serialize_config()/get_config()/put_config() (as of Task 7) and confirmed by live curl against a scratch-REPO_TRANSLATOR_HOME instance.
x-config-api-key-write-semantics.read_semantics: GET /config's translator object never contains the key 'api_key' at all (not present, not null, not empty string) -- only 'api_key_set': true|false, reflecting bool(stored_api_key).

write_semantics.omitted_or_null: payload.translator.api_key absent, or present with value null -- the stored key is left unchanged. This is how SettingsScreen saves every other field without forcing the user to re-paste an already-configured key. Implementation note: put_config() resolves this BEFORE Pydantic validation by substituting the previously-loaded current.translator.api_key into the payload.
write_semantics.empty_string: payload.translator.api_key == "" -- explicitly clears the stored key. Normalized server-side to None after validation (so api_key_set becomes false and the on-disk YAML omits the key entirely, same as a never-configured key) -- empty-string and unset are treated as the identical state, never stored as a literal "".
write_semantics.non_empty_string: payload.translator.api_key == "<value>" -- sets/replaces the stored key with that literal value.

x-config-api-key-write-semantics.frontend_round_trip_note: Because GET returns api_key_set (not api_key), and the frontend constructs its PUT body by spreading the last-fetched config object, every PUT naturally round-trips an 'api_key_set' key inside translator. This is harmless: AppConfig/TranslatorConfig are plain pydantic BaseModel subclasses with no ConfigDict(extra=...) override anywhere in config.py, so Pydantic v2's default extra='ignore' silently drops the unrecognized 'api_key_set' field during AppConfig.model_validate(payload) -- no explicit stripping needed server-side.
x-config-api-key-write-semantics.source_reference: repo_translator/api_server.py (_serialize_config(), get_config(), put_config()) and repo_translator/config.py (TranslatorConfig.api_key, no ConfigDict override present), as of Task 7 of docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-20-desktop-frontend.md. Verify against current source if this file appears stale.

x-ws-logs-contract._comment: Hand-documented because OpenAPI/Swagger has no schema coverage for WebSocket payloads. This route (WS /logs) is absent from the 'paths' object above for that reason -- FastAPI's openapi.json generation does not include @app.websocket routes at all. The shape below was read directly from repo_translator/api_server.py's _LogBroadcastHandler.emit method (captured 2026-06-20, re-verified unchanged on re-capture triggered by /health schema change; re-verified again 2026-06-21 when the GET /repos/{name}/files endpoint was added and the event/path/error optional fields below were introduced), not inferred or guessed.
x-ws-logs-contract.direction: server -> client only (the handler never reads incoming client messages for data; the server's logs_ws coroutine does call websocket.receive_text() in a loop, but purely to detect WebSocketDisconnect -- it ignores whatever text the client sends)
x-ws-logs-contract.message_format: One NDJSON (newline-delimited JSON) line per log record, sent as a WebSocket text frame via websocket.send_text(). Every logging.LogRecord emitted by the 'repo_translator' logger (and its children) while a client is connected is broadcast to every currently-connected client.

level.notes: Standard Python logging level names: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL.

message.notes: Already-formatted message (with %-style args substituted in); does not include exception tracebacks separately -- record.getMessage() only returns the log message text.

event.source: getattr(record, 'event', None) -- set via logging's extra= kwarg on specific call sites in repo_translator/sync.py (_process_one_file)
event.notes: Present only on the per-file sync log lines that pass extra={'event': ...}; absent (key omitted entirely, not null) from every other log line. This is the signal the desktop frontend's ReposScreen 'running' file-status overlay subscribes to.

path.source: getattr(record, 'path', None) -- same extra= mechanism as 'event', always present together with it
path.notes: Repo-relative file path the event/error refers to. Absent when 'event' is absent.

error.source: getattr(record, 'error', None) -- only set on event == 'file_failed' log lines
error.notes: Only present on file_failed events; absent on file_start/file_translated and on every plain (non-event) log line.

x-ws-logs-contract.example_line:
{"time": "2026-06-20T14:32:01.123456+00:00", "level": "INFO", "logger": "repo_translator.sync", "message": "Synced 3 files for repo 'example-repo'"} -- plain log line, no event/path/error keys.
{"time": "2026-06-21T10:00:00.000000+00:00", "level": "WARNING", "logger": "repo_translator.sync", "message": "Translation failed for 'docs/guide.md': timeout", "event": "file_failed", "path": "docs/guide.md", "error": "timeout"} -- per-file event line, additive fields present.
x-ws-logs-contract.source_reference: repo_translator/api_server.py, class _LogBroadcastHandler, method emit (as of commit at time of this snapshot). Verify against current source if this file appears stale -- see Step 3 staleness note in docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-20-desktop-frontend.md Task 0.5.
